PRODUCT SPECIALIST

Location: Ottawa, ON | Hybrid
Department: Product
Reports To: Chris Sisto, VP Product (Linkedin https://www.linkedin.com/in/chrissisto/)
Type: Permanent | Full-Time




ABOUT SOLINK

At Solink, our mission is to safeguard what matters most. We provide businesses with the tools to know sooner and act faster by transforming video security into real-time operational insights.

Our cloud-based platform integrates seamlessly with your existing cameras and systems, turning them into intelligent sensors that detect and interpret key moments. This empowers teams to make data-driven decisions, enhance security, and improve operational efficiency.

Trusted by over 30,000 locations across 32+ countries - including brands like McDonald’s and JYSK - Solink delivers clarity when it counts. Our solutions help businesses reduce shrink, optimize performance, and respond proactively to potential threats.

We're growing rapidly, earning industry recognition, and scaling with purpose. We’ve been recognized by Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, Business Intelligence Group, and as one of Ottawa’s Best Places to Work. And we’re just getting started!




THE ROLE

As a forward-deployed Product Specialist, you'll sit at the intersection of product, engineering, and customer success, helping bring Solink's platform to life in the real world. Think of this as a Forward Deployed role without the coding requirement: you're someone who understands the customer's world deeply, knows Solink's product inside and out, and has the curiosity and drive to solve problems at the edge of what our platform can do.

You'll work directly with customers and internal teams to deploy Solink's AI-powered features, build and configure custom agents, support model fine-tuning, and gather the datasets and feedback loops that help our AI get smarter over time.

This role is ideal for someone who is relentlessly curious, thrives in ambiguity, and finds energy in translating complex problems into practical solutions.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Customer & Product Deployment
   
   - Lead hands-on deployment of AI & AI Agent platforms with customers across commercial and enterprise segments, taking ownership from kickoff through to realized value with special projects
   
   - Troubleshoot and triage product deployment issues in real time, coordinating across teams to identify root causes and restore functionality quickly.
   
   - Translate customer operational challenges into working product configurations, working alongside Product to pressure-test assumptions and surface what's actually needed in the field.
   
   - Partner with Design, Customer Success, Sales, and Engineering to ensure smooth onboarding and fast time-to-value.
   
   - Document deployment patterns, edge cases, and playbooks that can be scaled across the team and fed back into product thinking and onboarding.
   
   AI Agent Configuration & Customization
   
   - Build, configure, and fine-tune custom AI agents within Solink's platform to solve specific customer use cases, working hand-in-hand with the AI and Product teams to define what's possible and what's worth building.
   
   - Collaborate closely with Engineering to understand agent architecture, provide real-world feedback on agent behavior, and help shape how agents evolve.
   
   - Validate agent performance against live customer scenarios, iterating based on field feedback and performance data.
   
   - Configure and validate how agent outputs are delivered and acted upon, including integrations and actions such as webhooks, email notifications, SMS, talk-down, and other downstream systems.
   
   - Maintain a growing library of reusable agent configurations and templates that make future deployments faster and smarter.

Location: Ottawa or Toronto, ON | Hybrid
Department: Engineering
Reports To: Dan Murdock, Director of Engineering
https://www.linkedin.com/in/dan-murdock/ Type: Full-Time
Vacancy Status: This is an active role and we are currently hiring for this position.



ABOUT SOLINK

At Solink, our mission is to safeguard what matters most. We provide businesses with the tools to know sooner and act faster by transforming video security into real-time operational insights.

Our cloud-based platform integrates seamlessly with your existing cameras and systems, turning them into intelligent sensors that detect and interpret key moments. This empowers teams to make data-driven decisions, enhance security, and improve operational efficiency.

Trusted by over 30,000 locations across 32+ countries - including brands like McDonald’s and JYSK - Solink delivers clarity when it counts. Our solutions help businesses reduce shrink, optimize performance, and respond proactively to potential threats.

We're growing rapidly, earning industry recognition, and scaling with purpose. We’ve been recognized by Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, Business Intelligence Group, and as one of Ottawa’s Best Places to Work. And we’re just getting started!




THE ROLE

On a small, high-velocity Agents team with outsized impact, this role is for a Full Stack Engineer who wants to build how AI agents actually show up in production.

You will design and implement the product-side systems that allow Solink’s AI Agents to reason, act, and deliver value inside our cloud VMS platform. This includes backend services (TypeScript/Node), agent orchestration layers, APIs, tool integrations, and user-facing workflows that turn models into real, reliable capabilities.

This is not a feature-factory role. You are building the execution layer of our agent systems — tool use, workflow coordination, safety boundaries, latency management, and production reliability.

This is a high-ownership role. You are expected to move quickly, ship working systems, and make strong implementation decisions while collaborating closely with product, data, and platform teams.

You will have direct access to the latest AI models and tooling, with the autonomy to apply them pragmatically where they create measurable product impact.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Implement and evolve agent orchestration services in TypeScript/Node

 - Design and ship production-grade APIs and workflows that expose agent capabilities

 - Integrate LLM/VLM systems with internal tools, permissions, and data sources

 - Enforce safety, guardrails, and failure handling in agent execution paths

 - Optimize latency, reliability, and scalability of agent-facing services in AWS/EKS

 - Instrument systems for tracing, debugging, and performance visibility

 - Operate in tight feedback loops, shipping working code and validating ideas quickly




WHAT YOU’LL BRING


MUST-HAVES

 - Strong TypeScript / Node.js experience building and operating production systems

 - Experience deploying and running distributed services in AWS (EKS preferred)

 - Experience integrating LLMs / VLMs into production workflows

 - Strong understanding of distributed systems (reliability, scaling, failure isolation)

 - Experience instrumenting systems for observability and debugging (OpenTelemetry or similar)

 - Demonstrated ownership of end-to-end product-facing systems in production


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience building AI agent frameworks or orchestration layers

 - Experience implementing safety controls and guardrails around model execution

 - Experience designing event-driven or tool-based architectures

 - Experience working in a small, high-velocity startup or AI-first environment


SECURITY REQUIREMENTS

 - Candidates must undergo a criminal records check upon hire;

 - Be a Canadian Citizen (dual citizens included), or eligible to work in Canada;

 - Be willing to comply with Solink’s own security policies and standards.

THE ROLE

Solink is looking for an Accounting Manager to own and evolve our end-to-end financial reporting function. You're not just executing the close, you're the person who designs how the close, the reconciliations, and the audit process should work. You will build the systems, judgment, and people-guidance that make those outcomes repeatable without you in the room.

You'll operate across revenue (IFRS 15), inventory (IAS 2), and fixed assets (IAS 16) in a hybrid SaaS and hardware-enabled environment, and you'll be looked to as the technical accounting authority and a trusted advisor to the Assistant Controller and Controller on complex, high-stakes decisions.

The ideal candidate is a CPA with a strong technical foundation, several years past the "learning the ropes" stage, who is energized by ambiguity, comfortable making principled trade-offs under constraints, and motivated by building durable systems, not just closing the books faster this month.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Financial Reporting & IFRS Compliance

 - Own the integrity of the full P&L and balance sheet, not just individual accounts

 - Prepare and review monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements in accordance with IFRS

 - Research and document accounting positions across IFRS 15, IAS 2, and IAS 16, and be the point person others escalate ambiguous transactions to

 - Translate complex or evolving transactions into clear, supportable, well-documented accounting treatments

 - Continuously evolve financial reporting practices for quality, accuracy, and scalability as the business grows in complexity

Audit & Compliance

 - Act as the primary point of contact for external auditors during interim and year-end audits

 - Own audit readiness as a system, not a scramble; build documentation and controls that hold up under scrutiny with minimal last-minute effort

 - Proactively identify structural audit risks before they surface in fieldwork, and address root causes rather than one-off findings

Revenue & Deferred Revenue Accounting

 - Own deferred revenue accounting end-to-end: completeness, accuracy, and cut-off

 - Ensure revenue recognition stays compliant with IFRS 15 as contract structures evolve, working ahead of the business rather than reacting to it

 - Partner with Billing and FP&A to validate billing schedules and contract modifications

 - Identify and drive automation opportunities in revenue tracking and reporting, reducing reliance on manual review

TECHNICAL TALENT PARTNER

 Location: Canada | Remote
Department: Talent Acquisition
Reports To: Grace Barbosa-Chin, Senior Director, People & Culture
Type: Permanent | Full-Time




ABOUT SOLINK

At Solink, our mission is to safeguard what matters most. We provide businesses with the tools to know sooner and act faster by transforming video security into real-time operational insights.

Our cloud-based platform integrates seamlessly with your existing cameras and systems, turning them into intelligent sensors that detect and interpret key moments. This empowers teams to make data-driven decisions, enhance security, and improve operational efficiency.

Trusted by over 30,000 locations across 32+ countries - including brands like McDonald’s and JYSK - Solink delivers clarity when it counts. Our solutions help businesses reduce shrink, optimize performance, and respond proactively to potential threats.

We're growing rapidly, earning industry recognition, and scaling with purpose. We’ve been recognized by Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, Business Intelligence Group, and as one of Ottawa’s Best Places to Work. And we’re just getting started!




THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Technical Talent Partner to help build and scale our R&D and Product organizations. This role goes beyond recruiting; you’ll act as a strategic partner to Engineering and Product leaders, shaping how we attract, assess, and hire exceptional talent.

You’ll work closely with the People Operations team and partner strategically with the P&C Business Partner for R&D and Product to align hiring with broader people strategies, workforce planning, and organizational design.

While your primary focus will be on R&D and Product, you’ll also have the opportunity to support hiring across other functions as business needs evolve. This requires adaptability, strong prioritization, and the ability to quickly ramp across different talent domains.

You’ll own full-cycle recruiting while influencing talent strategy and hiring excellence across the business. This is a high-impact role for someone who thrives in fast-paced, high-demand environments and is excited by the challenge of building both teams and scalable processes from the ground up.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Strategic Talent Partnership
   
   - Partner closely with Engineering and Product leaders to understand hiring needs, team structure, and long-term talent strategy
   
   - Work in close alignment with the P&C Business Partner for R&D and Product to ensure hiring plans align with org design and people priorities
   
   - Collaborate with the People Operations team to ensure consistency across hiring practices, processes, and systems
   
   - Translate business goals into proactive hiring plans and pipelines
   
   - Act as a trusted advisor on talent mapping, hiring decisions, and market insights
   
   Full-Cycle Recruiting Ownership
   
   - Own end-to-end recruitment for R&D and Product roles (Engineering, Product Management, Design, Data, etc)
   
   - Support hiring across other functions as needed, adapting quickly to different role profiles and hiring dynamics
   
   - Manage a high volume of requisitions while maintaining quality and strong stakeholder communication
   
   - Lead intake, sourcing strategy, structured interviews, offer negotiation, and closing
   
   - Ensure a high-quality, consistent, and efficient candidate experience
   
   Technical & Product Talent Sourcing
   
   - Build and execute creative sourcing strategies for hard-to-fill technical and product roles
   
   - Develop deep understanding of talent markets, competitors, and compensation trends
   
   - Proactively pipeline passive candidates for critical and future roles
   
   Hiring Excellence & Process Optimization
   
   - Drive structured hiring practices, including calibrated interviews and scorecards
   
   - Build, refine, and scale recruiting processes in a fast-paced, evolving environment
